// <auto-generated>This file has been auto generated from 'Qyl.OpenTelemetry.SemanticConventions.SourceGeneration'.</auto-generated>

#nullable enable

#pragma warning disable CS1570 // XML comment has badly formed XML

namespace OpenTelemetry.SemanticConventions;

/// <summary>
/// Constants for event names and typed payload structs outlined by the OpenTelemetry specifications.
/// </summary>
static partial class SessionIncubatingEvents
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Indicates that a session has ended.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// For instrumentation that tracks user behavior during user sessions, a `session.end` event SHOULD be emitted every time a session ends. When a session ends and continues as a new session, this event SHOULD be emitted prior to the `session.start` event.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    public const string EventSessionEnd = "session.end";

    /// <summary>
    /// Indicates that a new session has been started, optionally linking to the prior session.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// For instrumentation that tracks user behavior during user sessions, a `session.start` event MUST be emitted every time a session is created. When a new session is created as a continuation of a prior session, the `session.previous_id` SHOULD be included in the event. The values of `session.id` and `session.previous_id` MUST be different.
    /// When the `session.start` event contains both `session.id` and `session.previous_id` fields, the event indicates that the previous session has ended. If the session ID in `session.previous_id` has not yet ended via explicit `session.end` event, then the consumer SHOULD treat this continuation event as semantically equivalent to `session.end(session.previous_id)` and `session.start(session.id)`.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    public const string EventSessionStart = "session.start";

    /// <summary>
    /// Indicates that a session has ended.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// For instrumentation that tracks user behavior during user sessions, a `session.end` event SHOULD be emitted every time a session ends. When a session ends and continues as a new session, this event SHOULD be emitted prior to the `session.start` event.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    public static partial class SessionEndDescriptor
    {
        public const string Name = "session.end";
        public const string Brief = "Indicates that a session has ended.\n";
        public const string Note = "For instrumentation that tracks user behavior during user sessions, a `session.end` event SHOULD be emitted every time a session ends. When a session ends and continues as a new session, this event SHOULD be emitted prior to the `session.start` event.\n";
        public const string EmissionTarget = "unspecified";
        public const bool HasBody = false;
        public const string BodyJson = "";
        public const int AttributeCount = 1;
        public const int EntityAssociationCount = 0;
        public const string AttributeSessionId = "session.id";
        public const string AttributeSessionIdRequirementLevel = "required";
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Indicates that a new session has been started, optionally linking to the prior session.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    /// <remarks>
    /// For instrumentation that tracks user behavior during user sessions, a `session.start` event MUST be emitted every time a session is created. When a new session is created as a continuation of a prior session, the `session.previous_id` SHOULD be included in the event. The values of `session.id` and `session.previous_id` MUST be different.
    /// When the `session.start` event contains both `session.id` and `session.previous_id` fields, the event indicates that the previous session has ended. If the session ID in `session.previous_id` has not yet ended via explicit `session.end` event, then the consumer SHOULD treat this continuation event as semantically equivalent to `session.end(session.previous_id)` and `session.start(session.id)`.
    ///
    /// </remarks>
    public static partial class SessionStartDescriptor
    {
        public const string Name = "session.start";
        public const string Brief = "Indicates that a new session has been started, optionally linking to the prior session.\n";
        public const string Note = "For instrumentation that tracks user behavior during user sessions, a `session.start` event MUST be emitted every time a session is created. When a new session is created as a continuation of a prior session, the `session.previous_id` SHOULD be included in the event. The values of `session.id` and `session.previous_id` MUST be different.\nWhen the `session.start` event contains both `session.id` and `session.previous_id` fields, the event indicates that the previous session has ended. If the session ID in `session.previous_id` has not yet ended via explicit `session.end` event, then the consumer SHOULD treat this continuation event as semantically equivalent to `session.end(session.previous_id)` and `session.start(session.id)`.\n";
        public const string EmissionTarget = "unspecified";
        public const bool HasBody = false;
        public const string BodyJson = "";
        public const int AttributeCount = 2;
        public const int EntityAssociationCount = 0;
        public const string AttributeSessionId = "session.id";
        public const string AttributeSessionIdRequirementLevel = "required";
        public const string AttributeSessionPreviousId = "session.previous_id";
        public const string AttributeSessionPreviousIdRequirementLevel = "conditionally_required";
        public const string AttributeSessionPreviousIdRequirementCondition = "If the new session is being created as a continuation of a previous session, the `session.previous_id` SHOULD be included in the event. The `session.id` and `session.previous_id` attributes MUST have different values.\n";
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Indicates that a session has ended.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    public readonly record struct SessionEndPayload
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// The ID of the session being ended.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// Requirement level: required.
        /// </remarks>
        public string SessionId { get; init; }
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Indicates that a new session has been started, optionally linking to the prior session.
    ///
    /// </summary>
    public readonly record struct SessionStartPayload
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// The ID of the new session being started.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// Requirement level: required.
        /// </remarks>
        public string SessionId { get; init; }

        /// <summary>
        /// The previous `session.id` for this user, when known.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>
        /// Requirement level: conditionally_required.
        /// If the new session is being created as a continuation of a previous session, the `session.previous_id` SHOULD be included in the event. The `session.id` and `session.previous_id` attributes MUST have different values.
        ///
        /// </remarks>
        public string? SessionPreviousId { get; init; }
    }
}
